message.content:
Mount ₢BOAAC (heat ₣BO, srjcl-jupyter-reverify). The rbnnh_ per-nameplate customization
  mechanism just landed (4 commits, see `git log --oneline -10`) but the end-to-end
  notebook→Anthropic path is not working. Resume debugging.

  ## Symptom
  <REPLACE WITH WHAT YOU OBSERVE — exact step that failed, exact error/traceback from the
  notebook cell, whether the URL or warning behaved correctly, whether ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
  visibly reaches the bottle. If you don't know, run a notebook cell that calls Anthropic
  and paste the full traceback here.>

  ## What was built
  - `Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh` — `zrbob_kindle` declares `ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK`;
    `rbob_charge` invokes it after the readiness wait with `|| buc_die` exit-prop.
    Compose fragment path is now `rbnnh_compose.yml`.
  - `.rbk/srjcl/rbnnh_compose.yml` — bind-mounts `./srjcl/workspace` into
    `${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}` and forwards `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` bare-name from the
    launch shell.
  - `.rbk/srjcl/rbnnh_post_charge.sh` — host-side hook, self-contained (no BUK/RBK
    deps). Emits Jupyter URL; yellow warning if shell lacks `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.
  - `.rbk/srjcl/workspace/` — restored from `30a8e69a^:RBM-environments-srjcl/*`
    (claude-netdiags.ipynb, ipycl-name-genie.ipynb, namegenie.py, netdiag.py).
  - Spec: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSCH-charge_hook.adoc` plus RBS0/RBSCC wiring.

  ## Locked decisions (don't revisit unless I ask)
  - Hook must be self-contained — no BUK/RBK dependencies.
  - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` arrives via shell env only, never a station-files secrets file.
  - Hook nonzero exit propagates (failed hook = failed charge).
  - Single `rbnnh_` prefix covers both compose fragment and hook.
  - Bare-name compose `environment: - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is the sole key transport.

  ## First diagnostics to consider
  - After setting your shell key, re-charge srjcl
    (`./tt/rbw-cQ.Quench.srjcl.sh && ./tt/rbw-cC.Charge.srjcl.sh`) so the new value
    is picked up — env-passthrough resolves at compose-up time.
  - `docker exec canrbhl-srjcl-bottle printenv ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — does the key
    reach the bottle?
  - `.rbk/srjcl/rbrn.env` declares `RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=anthropic.com` and
    CIDR `160.79.104.0/23` — confirm egress reaches the API from the bottle.
  - Full notebook traceback is the most valuable signal.

  If the symptom section is blank, ask me before guessing.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Commit    Identity  Subject

93644530  ₢BOAAC    Refine charge cleanup message to name the actual cause of the wait: the implicit quench of a preexisting crucible is what can take a couple of minutes, not the cleanup of stray state in general.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
da390c6f  ₢BOAAC    Annotate charge cleanup step to set operator expectations on duration: docker compose down with --remove-orphans on a previously charged crucible can stall on container stop signals for a couple of minutes, and the bare 'Cleaning up any prior state' line gave no hint the wait was expected rather than a hang.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
7e212aa8  ₢BOAAC    Document the rbnnh_ per-nameplate customization mechanism. New RBSCH-charge_hook.adoc subdoc defines the two concepts (rbnnh_compose_fragment, rbnnh_post_charge_hook) with their detection, environment, and exit-status contract. RBS0 mapping section gains a Nameplate Customization comment block with both new quoins; body gains a Nameplate Customization Internals section between Foundry Internals and Trade Studies that anchors and includes RBSCH. RBSCC charge operation gains a new step describing optional hook invocation, references the new quoins, and states that nonzero hook exit propagates through charge. RBK acronyms file gains RBNNH and RBSCH entries pointing at the new prefix family and subdoc respectively.                              
708beb51  ₢BOAAC    Add optional rbnnh_ per-nameplate customization mechanism: rbnnh_compose.yml Compose overlay and rbnnh_post_charge.sh post-charge hook auto-detected in .rbk/MONIKER/. Hook runs as last charge step with full RBRN/RBRR exported; nonzero hook exit propagates through charge. Renamed compose fragments across srjcl/tadmor/ccyolo/moriah from bare compose.yml. srjcl gets the first hook emitting JupyterLab URL and yellow ANTHROPIC_API_KEY warning when absent; srjcl rbnnh_compose.yml adds an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY bare-name environment passthrough so shell-exported key reaches the bottle with no on-disk credential file. Also restored legacy srjcl baked-in workspace files under .rbk/srjcl/workspace/, surfaced into the bottle via the new compose bind mount.

content.text:
Mounted ₢BOAAC (srjcl-jupyter-reverify, heat ₣BO/rbk-11-mvp-tactical). The four landed commits exactly match what your prompt describes — rbnnh_ mechanism, spec wiring, and two cleanup-message refinements.

**Pace goal**: Confirm srjcl JupyterLab still works end-to-end and that a notebook cell can reach the Anthropic API through the bottle.

**Working tree note**: `.rbk/srjcl/workspace/ipycl-name-genie.ipynb` is modified locally — likely from a prior run leaving cell outputs/state. Worth checking before re-running to know what's a fresh signal vs stale state.

The symptom section in your prompt is the placeholder template (`<REPLACE WITH WHAT YOU OBSERVE — ...>`), not actual observations. Per your own instruction at the bottom — "If the symptom section is blank, ask me before guessing" — I'm stopping here rather than fishing.

Before I dive in, what did you actually see?

questions.question: Where did the end-to-end notebook→Anthropic path fail?

options.label: Charge itself failed
options.description: rbw-cC.Charge.srjcl.sh returned nonzero, or the post-charge hook fired an error / didn't emit the Jupyter URL

options.label: Key didn't reach bottle
options.description: Charge succeeded but `docker exec canrbhl-srjcl-bottle printenv ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` shows empty / missing

options.label: Notebook cell raised
options.description: Jupyter opened, key is present in bottle, but the cell calling Anthropic threw an exception — auth, network, SDK, etc.

options.label: JupyterLab didn't open
options.description: Couldn't reach the JupyterLab URL at all (port mapping, sentry blocking, URL emitted wrong, etc.)

questions.question: What should I have in hand before guessing?

options.label: Full notebook traceback
options.description: Paste the exception text from the failing cell — most diagnostic signal

options.label: printenv result from bottle
options.description: Output of `docker exec canrbhl-srjcl-bottle printenv ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (truthful presence/absence, no need to share value)

options.label: Charge tail (last ~80 lines)

options.label: Local working tree status
options.description: Confirm whether the .ipynb modification is stale state from a prior run or fresh from this attempt

content.text:
Found it. The traceback was already in the notebook outputs:

```
NotFoundError: Error code: 404 - {'type': 'error', 'error': {'type': 'not_found_error', 
  'message': 'model: claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620'}, 'request_id': 'req_011Cb4YoJxt6XmY1en8xNwr6'}
```

This is excellent news for the rbnnh_ mechanism — it's working end-to-end:

1. **API key reached the bottle** → otherwise this would be 401, not 404
2. **Network egress works** → the request landed at Anthropic's API
3. **The 404 is just stale model ID** in legacy notebook code

The failure is at `/workspace/namegenie.py:162`: `model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620"` (retired model).

Let me look at namegenie.py to see what models it uses.

Single site to fix. Both the commented-out haiku and the active sonnet ID are retired.

## Summary

**Symptom**: `NotFoundError: 404 — model: claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620` from notebook cell `In[5]` (`namegenie.name_help`).

**What this tells us**:
- ✅ `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` reached the bottle (401 would mean missing/bad key)
- ✅ Bottle egress to `api.anthropic.com` is fine (request got to Anthropic and came back)
- ✅ rbnnh_ mechanism is fully working
- ❌ Legacy `.rbk/srjcl/workspace/namegenie.py:162` hardcodes a retired model ID

**Proposed repair**: Update `.rbk/srjcl/workspace/namegenie.py:162` from `claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620` to a current model. The notebook generates witty word lists — Sonnet quality fits the original intent.

options.label: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
options.description: Latest Haiku — cheaper/faster; the commented-out original was haiku, so this honors the older preference

content.text:
Yes — new errors, but it's progress. The 404 (retired model) is gone; now we hit a 400:

```
BadRequestError: 400 — invalid_request_error
'This model does not support assistant message prefill. 
 The conversation must end with a user message.'
```

The code at namegenie.py:170-172 uses the old "assistant prefill" trick — ending the message list with `{"role": "assistant", "content": "Your words are:"}` so the model continues from that prefix. Claude 4.x doesn't allow that; the last message must be `user`.

Let me see the exact lines.

content.content:

166	        Terse, offering no explanations, you provide a list of interesting words
167	        that match the theme. 
168	        """,
169	        messages=[
170	            {"role": "user", "content": composite_hint },
171	            {"role": "assistant", "content": "Your words are:"}  # Prefill
172	        ]
173	    )

175

file.content:
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    message = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=1000,
        temperature=temperature,
        system="""
        You are a brilliant and witty selector of words with really good gestalts.
        Terse, offering no explanations, you provide a list of interesting words
        that match the theme. 
        """,
        messages=[
            {"role": "user", "content": composite_hint },
            {"role": "assistant", "content": "Your words are:"}  # Prefill
        ]
    )
    content = message.content[0].text.strip().split('\n')

    valid_words, rejected_words = filter_and_sort_names(content, min_letters, max_letters)

    mkd = []
    mkd += ['## Valid words']

If the response now contains a preamble line ("Here are some words:") before the list, we'll need a stronger system-prompt directive — but worth trying this minimal form first.

input.params: {"identity": "BOAAC", "files": [".rbk/srjcl/workspace/namegenie.py", ".rbk/srjcl/workspace/ipycl-name-genie.ipynb"], "intent": "Drop assistant-message prefill from srjcl name-genie helper: claude-sonnet-4-6 rejects conversations that end on an assistant turn with 400 invalid_request_error ('This model does not support assistant message prefill. The conversation must end with a user message.'). The 'Your words are:' priming text is folded into the trailing user message so the model still gets the start-with-the-list signal without violating the new API constraint. Notebook captures the 400 trace from the prior attempt that motivated this fix."}

content.text:
Notched as `8e5a6aef`. Working tree clean.

## Self-Assessment of ₢BOAAC (srjcl-jupyter-reverify)

**Pace goal (from docket)**: Reverify the srjcl jupyter notebook still functions and can reach Anthropic.

**Verdict**: Goal met. Full path is working — shell-env API key → bottle → sentry egress → Anthropic API → notebook cell rendering a word list.

### What landed under this pace (7 commits total)
| Commit | Theme |
|---|---|
| `708beb51` | rbnnh_ mechanism: compose overlay + post-charge hook (auto-detected, host-side, self-contained, exit-prop), srjcl workspace restored from history |
| `7e212aa8` | Spec wiring: RBSCH subdoc + RBS0/RBSCC linkage + RBK acronym table |
| `da390c6f` | Charge cleanup messaging: duration expectation |
| `93644530` | Charge cleanup messaging: name the actual cause |
| `9b82abdd` | Retire stale `claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620` → `claude-sonnet-4-6` |
| `db9ed5a6` | Drop assistant-message prefill (4.x API constraint), fold priming into user message |
| `8e5a6aef` | Capture green notebook outputs |

### What this revealed beyond the docket
The pace's stated character was "mechanical with one design beat" — the design beat being "consider surfacing the local port during crucible charge so the operator doesn't have to fish for it." That design beat got built (the post-charge hook emits the JupyterLab URL) and generalized into the rbnnh_ mechanism — substantially more than a single-nameplate URL surface. That's a scope expansion that earned its keep: it gives every future nameplate a clean extension point.

### Honest concerns / spooks worth naming
1. **Notebook size in commits** — both recent notches tripped the 30KB warning. The .ipynb carries cell outputs (tracebacks, success content) inline. If srjcl gets exercised regularly under this pace's successor, the warnings will keep firing. Possible itch: scrub cell outputs before commit, or raise threshold for srjcl workspace, or rely on `.ipynb_checkpoints` and commit a stripped version. **Not in scope for this pace** — flagging for an itch.
2. **No automated regression** — this whole verification was operator-eyeball. A future regression in the rbnnh_ wiring or model deprecation would only surface when someone next runs the notebook. The `srjcl-jupyter` test fixture mentioned in CLAUDE.md (3 cases) likely doesn't cover the Anthropic round-trip. **Not in scope** — flagging for an itch.
3. **The "one design beat" got expanded** — fine that it did, but worth noting the docket undersold the scope. Pace honestly took on the rbnnh_ mechanism design, not just URL surfacing. Future paddock-prose could reflect this if you care.

### Ready to wrap?
Per JJK discipline I'm not auto-wrapping. When you confirm "wrap ₢BOAAC" I'll run `jjx_close` with a summary — and you can decide whether either of the spooks above merits a fresh pace before this heat continues to its next actionable (₢BOAAA gauntlet shakedown).

input.params: {"coronet": "BOAAC", "summary": "srjcl jupyter notebook reverified end-to-end: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY transports from the launch shell through a bare-name compose environment passthrough into the bottle, sentry egress reaches api.anthropic.com, and the name-genie cell returns word-list content. Scope expanded from the original docket's 'surface the local port during charge' design beat into a general rbnnh_ per-nameplate customization mechanism: rbnnh_compose.yml Compose overlay fragment and rbnnh_post_charge.sh host-side hook, both auto-detected in .rbk/MONIKER/, hook runs as last charge step with full RBRN/RBRR exported and nonzero exit propagating. Spec wired through RBSCH subdoc with RBS0/RBSCC/RBK-acronym linkage. srjcl is the first nameplate to use the mechanism — its hook emits the JupyterLab URL and prints a yellow warning if the launch shell lacks ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Legacy srjcl baked-in workspace files restored from history under .rbk/srjcl/workspace/. Two notebook fixes landed during reverification: retired the deprecated claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620 model ID (replaced with claude-sonnet-4-6) and dropped the assistant-message prefill (Claude 4.x rejects conversations not ending on a user turn), folding the 'Your words are:' priming into the trailing user message. Charge cleanup messaging refined to set operator expectations on the multi-minute implicit-quench wait. Two spooks surfaced and worth itching later: notebook-output commit size pressure (tripped 30KB warning twice), and lack of automated regression coverage for the notebook→Anthropic round trip."}
